subject: Public Health
title: The Sewer Revolution
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Sewer Revolution.

hook.text: London's Great Stink of 1858 forced Parliament to fund sewers—the smell from the Thames was unbearable.

buildup.text: Joseph Bazalgette designed 1,100 miles of sewers that redirected waste away from drinking water sources.

discovery.text: Sewer systems cut cholera, typhoid, and dysentery rates dramatically in every city that built them.

twist.text: Most cities in low-income countries still lack sewer systems—3.6 billion people use unsafe sanitation.

climax.text: Decentralized systems—like septic tanks and container-based sanitation—offer alternatives where sewers aren't feasible.

punchline.text: The sewer is invisible—and invisibly saves millions of lives.

quiz.question: What event forced London to build its sewer system?
